The IT Infrastructure & Operations group is responsible for managing and maintaining ESS data centres, private cloud infrastructure, servers and computing resources, storage and backup systems, network management, internet connectivity, wireless and mobile networks, identity and access management, and IT security.



As an Identity and Access Management (IAM) summer worker, your main tasks will be to assist with regression testing of key user scenarios in the One Identity Web Portal, with a particular focus on joiner and registration workflows, and to carry out exploratory testing of the Web Portal and IT Shop from an end-user perspective.



You will help review the visibility and presentation of information in the Web Portal, document testing results and recommendations in a structured manner, and support the development and update of end-user knowledge base material such as step-by-step guides, screenshots, tips and articles.



You will also assist with service desk support activities related to the access object lifecycle, help collect and organise information for onboarding new access objects and services, and support supervised improvements to IAM-related workflows, user guidance and service request processes.


**About you**  

A suitable background for this position would be:


* Enrolled at university in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering or a related field
* An interest in identity and access management, usability testing, service processes or end-user support
* Experience with web applications, documentation, testing or basic front-end technologies would be beneficial
* Good written and oral communication skills in English
* Minimum age 18 years


We believe that you enjoy analysing how services work from an end-user perspective and collaborating with a variety of people in an international environment. You are observant, methodical and able to describe findings clearly in writing. We also value initiative, sound judgement and a willingness to ask questions when dealing with business-critical access processes.

title: Social Media Manager
description:
### **The role**


In your full-time role as a **Social Media Manager,** you will work closely alongside our Brand Manager to operate the social media presence of our current and future Formula 1 drivers.


This is a role with real ambition behind it. You’ll be responsible for collating and organising content, developing posts, sourcing audio, monitoring trends and reporting on what's happening across the social landscape; from competitor drivers to leading athletes.


You’ll also be responsible for creating a strategy for wearegrip’s social accounts and implementing it alongside our wider social team. As you grow into the role and demonstrate your strongunderstanding of social media, our drivers and their audiences, the goal is for you to take full end-to-end ownership of multiple racing driver and/or athlete accounts.


We're a small team, so it's essential that you're self-motivated and have the passion and determination to continually grow and evolve your skill set. You will often have the opportunity to work on wider campaigns that exist beyond social, and you'll work collaboratively across a range of projects within wearegrip.

### **About us**


We are **wearegrip**, a London-based creative studio who work with some of the most exciting drivers in F1. Our goal is to build the most distinctive personal brands in sport that capture the attention of fans, teams, and brands around the world.


Working hand-in-hand with each driver, we create and manage their personal brand across social media, branding, marketing, merchandise, content creation, digital, and more. When you join wearegrip, you’ll be working at the heart of our multi-skilled, fast-paced start-up and immediately have the opportunity to begin shaping your future within our highly motivated and passionate team.

### **About you**

* You are based in London and can commute to East London at least 4 times a week.
* You have at least 3 years of experience in a Social Media role, ideally within sport.
* You have experience working on high-profile social media accounts with an audience of 50,000+
* You have experience supporting or contributing to social media accounts with a large and engaged audience.
* You have a huge passion for motorsport and specifically Formula 1.
* You have a good understanding of best practices and emerging trends across the main social media networks (Instagram, TikTok, X, Threads, YouTube & Facebook).
* You have excellent English copywriting skills and a strong instinct for tone of voice.
* You're obsessed with social media, always watching what other athletes, drivers and creators are doing and thinking about why it works.
* You can commit to working a minimum of 20 weekends across the Formula 1 Calendar, with days off in the week for any weekends worked.
* You have a creative mind that continually looks to find new ways to be unique in the ever changing world of social media.

### **Your responsibilities (but not limited to)**

* Supporting the Brand Manager in the day-to-day running of athlete social media accounts.
* Collating and organising content assets from race weekends and beyond including video, photo, audio and more.
* Sourcing sounds and audio for use across social content.
* Researching and reporting on social media trends, competitor drivers and leading athletes/influencers to keep the team informed and inspired.
* Proactive community management and fan engagement across all platforms.
* Supporting partner campaign activations across required social channels.
* Monitoring motorsport news and relevant stories/moments.
* Caption writing and helping to bring a driver's tone of voice to life across platforms.
* Supporting the planning and posting of merchandise campaigns.
* Create a strategy for our wearegrip social accounts and implement it alongside the wider Social Media team.
* Capturing phone-style content for social channels when required.
* Briefing and liaising with designers, photographers and videographers as needed.

### **Your skills**

* Ability to creatively write in different tone of voices
* Basic knowledge of Adobe Creative Cloud software.
* Well-organised and efficient, with a willingness to learn and adapt in a fast-paced environment.
* Strong attention to detail.
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
* Ability to manage your own workload and flag priorities effectively.

### **Not essential but always a bonus**

* Previous experience in sport or motorsport social media.
* Experience working on partnership campaigns

### **Benefits**

* Competitive salary based on experience.
* 20 days of annual leave plus a minimum of 5 days leave over Christmas and New Year.
* 1 extra fully paid day off for your birthday.
* "Work from Anywhere" Wednesdays, mandatory working out of office day every week.
* Days in lieu when required to work weekends
* Additional work from home days available on request.
* Apple Macbook and phone plus any relevant software and equipment.
* Budget for personal skills training and development.
* Days in lieu when required to work a Grand Prix weekend.

### **How to apply**

**‍**If this role excites you and you're ready to take the next step in your career, here's how to apply:

* Write a brief overview explaining why you're perfect for the role and ready to join our team.
* Include your CV.
* Then email it all to **join@wearegrip.com** with the subject **"Social Media Manager"**.


We can't wait to hear from you!

title: Senior Manager, Sales Engineering – Visa Value Added Services (VAS), Issuing solutions (Pismo)
description:
**About Us**  

Visa is a world leader in payments technology, facilitating transactions between consumers, merchants, financial institutions and government entities across more than 200 countries and territories, dedicated to uplifting everyone, everywhere by being the best way to pay and be paid.


At Visa, you'll have the opportunity to create impact at scale — tackling meaningful challenges, growing your skills and seeing your contributions impact lives around the world.


Join Visa and do work that matters – to you, to your community, and to the world. Progress starts with you.

**Job Description**

**Overview**


We are seeking a Senior Sales Engineer who can bridge deep product knowledge with clear business outcomes for Visa’s Value-Added Services (VAS) across Europe. In this role, you will help banks, issuers, and fintechs understand how Visa’s Pismo platform fit into their architecture, design solutions that win enterprise trust, and collaborate closely with Sales, Product, and Engineering to accelerate adoption of AI‑native, cloud‑native issuing and digital solutions.


As part of Visa, Pismo is redefining the future of issuing and core payments. Pismo’s technology enables financial institutions to move beyond legacy constraints and deliver modern digital experiences at scale. Moreover, it plays a critical role in the broader ecosystem—its cloud‑native, real‑time issuing and core processing platform serves as a backbone and enabler for numerous Visa Value‑Added Services.


A key part of the role will be solutioning complex, multi‑product deals that combine Pismo with other Visa services, delivering unified architectures that maximize client value. You will guide clients through these combined solution designs, shaping end‑to‑end modernization programs that are scalable, secure, and implementation‑ready. This role requires both strategic leadership and hands‑on engagement, driving architectural clarity, influencing senior stakeholders, and helping major European institutions transform their technology landscapes.


This is a remote position. A remote position does not require job duties be performed within proximity of a Visa office location. Remote positions may be required to be present at a Visa office with scheduled notice.

**Qualifications**


8+ years of relevant work experience with a Bachelor’s Degree or at least 5 years of experience with an Advanced Degree (e.g. Masters, MBA, JD, MD), or 2 years of work experience with a PhD, or 11+ years of relevant work experience. Experience in solutions architecture or technical sales engineering, ideally in payments, fintech, or high‑growth technology environments. Strong understanding of technical architectures, APIs, data flows, integrations, and cloud infrastructure. Consultative selling mindset: able to map Visa’s product capabilities to client pain points, regulatory needs, and measurable ROI. Skilled at simplifying complex technical concepts for both engineering teams and executive stakeholders. Builder mentality: creating reusable frameworks, demos, POCs, and scalable pre‑sales assets. Strong cross‑functional partner to Sales, Product, Engineering, and Delivery, maintaining tight feedback loops. Bonus: experience with AI platforms, developer tools, or enterprise integration patterns. Full fluency in an European Language and English**Visa is an EEO Employer**


Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability or protected veteran status. Visa will also consider for employment qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with EEOC guidelines and applicable local law.

title: Senior Site Reliability Engineer - Open Banking
description:
**About Us**  

Visa is a world leader in payments technology, facilitating transactions between consumers, merchants, financial institutions and government entities across more than 200 countries and territories, dedicated to uplifting everyone, everywhere by being the best way to pay and be paid.


At Visa, you'll have the opportunity to create impact at scale — tackling meaningful challenges, growing your skills and seeing your contributions impact lives around the world.


Join Visa and do work that matters – to you, to your community, and to the world. Progress starts with you.

**Job Description**


We are seeking a Site Reliability Engineer to Visa Open Banking - delivering open banking solutions as a part of Visa.


Perhaps the most dramatic outcome of the digital revolution is the amount of data that is now collected and analysed. Visa Open Banking handles billions of events, and this number is increasing at an exponential rate. We are passionate about enabling data-driven decisions for our clients and internal stakeholders. Our goal is to ensure data is collected, transformed, and presented securely with accuracy and reliability. Building the infrastructure and services to support this is hence a key part of what VOB engineers are doing.


We are looking for a Site Reliability Engineer to join our Stateful team. You will be part of the team responsible for maintaining and improving VOB system reliability, scalability and efficiency. Your primary focus will be on automating our operational activities and creating a bridge between operations and development.


What we offer you:


A position in the Stateful team means working in an international environment where you will be involved in:

* Providing VOB engineers with tailored self-service tools and services that enable teams to deliver, operate and observe their services, with low friction.
* Focusing on automation and tooling to enable other engineering teams to focus on core business activities.
* Automate provisioning, monitoring, and scaling using scripting (Python, Go, Bash).
* Collaborating with stakeholders to ensure that the services and tools align with their evolving requirements and use cases.
* Being a best practice champion, influence and create new designs, architectures, standards and methods for large-scale systems.


This is a hybrid position. Expectation of days in office will be confirmed by your hiring manager.

**Qualifications**


Basic Qualifications: 2 or more years of relevant work experience and a Bachelors degree, OR 5 or more years of relevant work experience Preferred Qualifications: 3 or more years of work experience with a Bachelors Degree or more than 2 years of work experience with an Advanced Degree (e.g. Masters, MBA, JD, MD). Proven experience in designing, building, and managing large-scale, dependable cloud services in close collaboration with diverse stakeholders. Proficiency in programming languages such as Java, Go or Python. Experience with cloud platforms like AWS, Google Cloud or Azure. A strong desire to foster teamwork and collaborative efforts. Working in an IaC environment using tooling such as Terraform, or Ansible. Experience with monitoring tools like Prometheus, Grafana, Datadog. Experience with GenAI tools for coding and day to day tasks. Strong communication skills and the ability to work as a part of the team. Ability to participate in an on-call rotation.**Visa is an EEO Employer**


Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability or protected veteran status. Visa will also consider for employment qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with EEOC guidelines and applicable local law.

title: Product Manager – User Platform
description:
**About Us**  

Visa is a world leader in payments technology, facilitating transactions between consumers, merchants, financial institutions and government entities across more than 200 countries and territories, dedicated to uplifting everyone, everywhere by being the best way to pay and be paid.


At Visa, you'll have the opportunity to create impact at scale — tackling meaningful challenges, growing your skills and seeing your contributions impact lives around the world.


Join Visa and do work that matters – to you, to your community, and to the world. Progress starts with you.

**Job Description**


Tink was founded in 2012, and a decade later became part of Visa in 2022. Tink was created with the aim of changing the banking industry for the better. We have built Europe’s most robust open banking platform – with the broadest, deepest connectivity and powerful services that create value out of the financial data. We offer the tools that allow anyone – from big banks and fintech to startups – to build the future of financial services across Europe.


The Product and Business Enablement area at Tink has a clear mission: to build the foundational platform services that power our entire product ecosystem. We are a tightly knit team with product managers, backend and frontend engineers, platform architects, and data specialists. By building robust, scalable, and well-designed platform services, we enable product teams across Tink to deliver exceptional value to customers and end users.  

What you will do:


We are looking for a Product Manager to own and drive the User Platform – a critical backend service that manages user identity, authentication, session management, and user lifecycle across Tink's product suite. This platform underpins how we identify, authenticate, and serve millions of end users around the world, providing the foundation for personalised experiences, security, and compliance.  

Responsibilities:

* Define and own the product roadmap for the User Platform, balancing technical debt, scalability requirements, and business needs
* Work closely with backend engineers to define requirements for APIs, data models, and service architectures
* Collaborate with product teams across Tink to understand their user identity and authentication requirements, translating these into platform capabilities
* Partner with security, compliance, and legal teams to ensure the platform meets regulatory requirements (GDPR, PSD2, etc.) and security standards
* Define service-level objectives (SLOs) in partnership with engineering**,** monitor platform health, and drive improvements to reliability, performance, and scalability
* Manage platform dependencies and integrations with third-party identity providers, authentication services, and internal systems
* Create clear technical documentation and API specifications to enable seamless integration by internal product teams
* Balance competing priorities from multiple stakeholders while maintaining platform stability and forward progress
* Drive data-informed decision-making by defining key metrics, monitoring platform usage, and analysing user behaviour patterns


This is a hybrid position. Expectation of days in office will be confirmed by your hiring manager.

**Qualifications**


We are looking for a technically minded product manager who can bridge the gap between engineering teams and business requirements. You should be comfortable working with backend systems, APIs, and platform architecture while maintaining a clear focus on delivering value to internal and external stakeholders. 3-5 years of product management experience at a technology company, with at least some of that time focused on platform, infrastructure, or backend services Strong technical background – you should be comfortable reading API documentation, understanding system architecture diagrams, and discussing technical trade-offs with engineers Experience with API design, service-oriented architectures, or platform development is highly desirable Understanding of authentication and authorisation concepts (OAuth, JWT, session management, etc.) Familiarity with identity management, user lifecycle management, or related domains is a plus Demonstrated ability to prioritise competing requirements and manage platform roadmaps Strong written and verbal communication skills – you'll be creating technical documentation, API specs, and stakeholder updates Data-driven decision-making approach with experience defining and tracking platform metrics Familiarity with distributed systems, microservices, or cloud infrastructure is beneficial**Visa is an EEO Employer**


Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability or protected veteran status. Visa will also consider for employment qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with EEOC guidelines and applicable local law.

title: Data Platform Architect
description:
**About Us**  

Visa is a world leader in payments technology, facilitating transactions between consumers, merchants, financial institutions and government entities across more than 200 countries and territories, dedicated to uplifting everyone, everywhere by being the best way to pay and be paid.


At Visa, you'll have the opportunity to create impact at scale — tackling meaningful challenges, growing your skills and seeing your contributions impact lives around the world.


Join Visa and do work that matters – to you, to your community, and to the world. Progress starts with you.

**Job Description**


As a Data Platform Architect within Visa’s Open Banking AI and Data Platform Domain, based in Stockholm, Sweden, you will lead the design, development, and implementation of our next-generation data platform. In this role, you’re responsible for driving the execution of the technical vision to deliver a secure, compliant, and resilient data ecosystem that powers a breadth of use cases, including (but not limited to) data warehousing, real-time and batch data processing, analytical events systems, advanced data modelling, and infrastructure management. You will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams across engineering, product, compliance, and AI to architect solutions that meet the demanding performance, scalability, security, and regulatory requirements of the Open Banking landscape. In this capacity, you will recommend and apply best-in-class architecture patterns, frameworks, and guidelines. You will set technical direction and mentor engineers to build high-quality, maintainable solutions that align with Visa’s strategic objectives.


This is a hybrid position. Expectation of days in office will be confirmed by your hiring manager.

**Qualifications**


Extensive experience in data architecture, including data warehousing, ETL/ELT pipelines, and data lakes within enterprise-grade environments. Proven track record in building secure, compliant, and resilient systems, with a deep understanding of security principles, privacy regulations (e.g. GDPR, PSD2). Expertise in data processing frameworks and programming languages (e.g. Spark, Hadoop, SQL, Python/Scala/Java) and analytical event streaming systems (e.g. Kafka, Kinesis). Strong background in advanced data modelling techniques, schema design, master data management, and metadata management. Hands-on experience managing cloud infrastructures (e.g. AWS, Azure, GCP), containerization (Docker, Kubernetes), and Infrastructure-as-Code (Terraform, CloudFormation). Familiarity with AI/ML data pipelines, enabling integration of machine learning models into production data workflows. Demonstrated ability to lead end-to-end technical projects, set architectural vision, drive consensus across stakeholders, and deliver on time. Exceptional communication, collaboration, and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to serve as a trusted advisor to senior leadership. Deep technical proficiency, strategic vision, providing mentorship and thought leadership, and engaging in high-level consultation across the organization. Relevant qualifications: Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Information Systems, or a related field. Professional certifications (e.g. TOGAF, AWS Certified Solutions Architect) preferred.**Visa is an EEO Employer**


Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability or protected veteran status. Visa will also consider for employment qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with EEOC guidelines and applicable local law.

title: Penetration Tester to Accenture Security
description:
**Be yourself.**  

**Make an impact on the future.**


Are you ready to take your expertise to the next level?


At Accenture, no two workdays are ever the same—and that’s exactly why we love it here! This is a great opportunity to build a rewarding career at Accenture Security. With us, you’ll work in a boldly relaxed atmosphere alongside talented colleagues who support your success. We nurture your individual strengths through an engaging work community where everyone can truly be themselves. At Accenture, you’ll get to work on client projects for some of the world’s leading companies.

**We are looking for a Security Tester to join our team**


You read in the news that a major company’s sensitive data has fallen into the hands of hackers. The article explains that the attacker used an HTTP request smuggling technique. By sending specially crafted HTTP requests, the attacker caused the web server and proxy to interpret the requests differently. This made it possible to bypass security controls and gain access to protected resources. As a result, the attacker was able to steal user data and launch denial-of-service attacks.


You sigh deeply and think that once again, the basics have been neglected. You continue working on a new, challenging security test. At the same time, you reflect on how you could be part of building more secure systems - you don’t want your friends’ or family’s data to end up in the wrong hands.


  

We’re looking for someone who lives and breathes IT security, with a particular passion for penetration testing. If you enjoy breaking things to make them better, this might be your next gig.

* **3+ years** of hands-on experience running and owning penetration tests from start to finish.
* Comprehensive expertise in **application and web application security testing**, including familiarity with the **OWASP Top 10**. Proficient in infrastructure domains such as Windows Active Directory, Linux systems, and network security.
* Demonstrated experience with leading cloud platforms including **AWS, GCP, and Azure.**
* **Fluent in both Swedish and English,** with strong written and verbal communication skills in each language.

### **Bonus points if you bring:**

* Solid experience in software development, AWS-native architectures, IaaS, or containerized microservices.
* Familiarity with the latest buzzwords that actually matter — because our clients use them too.
* Certifications like OSCP or other relevant security creds.

**As a Penetration Tester with us**, you're not just technically sharp — you also know how to communicate and share your knowledge with both clients and colleagues. You're a natural source of inspiration, constantly exploring new technologies and pushing your passion for security forward.

**What we offer**
-----------------

### **Events & Community**

* Team kickoffs and spontaneous after-work hangouts.
* Access to a wide variety of training and certification programs, including virtual and in-person courses tailored to professional and technical development.
* A culture of collaboration and curiosity, where knowledge-sharing is second nature.

### **Benefits That Back You Up**

* Choose your gear: PC, Mac or Linux.
* Private health insurance, life & accident coverage, pension, and travel insurance.
* 5,000 SEK/year wellness allowance.
* Accenture IF offers possible activities and sports for all our employees.

### **Flexibility**

* Hybrid work model.
* Flexible hours to match your rhythm.

**Interested?**  

Send us your CV and application by 28/2.


At Accenture, we believe that diverse people achieve greater things together than those who are all the same. That’s why we want you just as you are—regardless of your gender, ethnic background, culture, nationality, religion or beliefs, sexual orientation, or gender identity. Join us in continuing to build a diverse and inclusive workplace!


Accenture is a leading global professional services company, providing a broad range of services in strategy and consulting, interactive, technology, and operations, with digital capabilities across all of these services. We combine unmatched experience and specialized capabilities across more than 40 industries—powered by the world’s largest network of Advanced Technology and Intelligent Operations centers. With 791,000 people serving clients in more than 120 countries, Accenture brings continuous innovation to help clients improve their performance and create lasting value across their enterprises. Visit us at www.accenture.com.
company_description: Our 721,000 people deliver on the promise of technology and human ingenuity every day, serving clients in more than 120 countries. We embrace the power of change to create value and shared success.

title: Säljkoordinator till Nordic Wellness
description:
### **Säljkoordinator till Nordic Wellness försäljningsavdelning**

##### **Vill du vara med och göra skillnad för folkhälsan samtidigt som du stöttar affären bakom kulisserna? Nu har du chansen!**


Nordic Wellness är Sveriges största friskvårdskedja med **över 400 klubbar** och ett antal padelanläggningar runt om i landet.  

Det ger oss unika möjligheter att ständigt utveckla våra klubbar och att ligga i framkant när det gäller utbud, kvalitet, personal – och inte minst pris.


Vi erbjuder ett brett utbud av träning och välmående för alla åldrar, har närmare 550 000 medlemmar och fortsätter vår expansionsresa framåt. **Vårt mål är att nå 600 klubbar till år 2030.**


För att möta vår tillväxt söker vi nu en strukturerad, serviceinriktad och lösningsorienterad **Säljkoordinator** som vill vara en nyckelspelare i vårt säljteam.

**Rollen – vad du gör som Säljkoordinator**
-------------------------------------------


Som Säljkoordinator är du en central del av vår försäljningsavdelning och fungerar som spindeln i nätet. Du stöttar våra säljare runt om i Sverige och säkerställer att våra processer, avtal och affärssystem håller hög kvalitet.


Du arbetar nära vår försäljningschef, Head of Sports samt våra säljare i ett nationellt säljteam med cirka 20 engagerade kollegor. Rollen innebär både administrativt ansvar och löpande kontakt med kunder, samarbetspartners och interna funktioner. Till din hjälp har du befintliga 3 säljkoordinatorer samt försäljningschef som bollplank – allt för att du ska lyckas så bra som möjligt i din roll!

**Ditt uppdrag**
----------------

* Stötta säljteamet i det dagliga arbetet
* Hantera beställningar av medlemskap samt support via mejl och telefon
* Uppdatera och administrera avtal, erbjudanden och kundinformation
* Säkerställa att vårt affärssystem (BRP Cloud) är uppdaterat
* Vara bollplank till säljare i administrativa och kundrelaterade frågor
* Beställa material inför event och möten
* Delta i framtagning av sälj- och presentationsmaterial tillsammans med marknad
* Delta vid event och teamaktiviteter vid behov
* Driva viss egen försäljning

**Det här ingår också i din roll**
----------------------------------

* Träffa vårt säljteam varannan månad fysiskt för gemensam konferens och teambuilding
* Delta vid event tillsammans med föreningar vid behov
* Skapa och vara kreativ kring marknadsmaterial tillsammans med vår marknadsavdelning
* Bidra till ett gott samarbete mellan avdelningar


*Och självklart – mycket skratt, stark gemenskap och ibland härlig gemensam träning på gymmet!*

**Vi söker dig som**
--------------------


Vi söker dig som är en lagspelare men samtidigt kan ta stort eget ansvar. Du är målmedveten, utåtriktad, positiv och har ett driv i att utveckla försäljningen och stötta teamet framåt.


Du är engagerad, strukturerad, kreativ och lösningsorienterad, samtidigt som du har ett öga för vad varje säljare och kund behöver och kan anpassa dig efter det. Du trivs i ett högt tempo med många kontaktytor och har ett stort fokus på service, kvalitet och ett professionellt bemötande.


Din sociala kompetens är en av dina största styrkor och du förstår vikten av att bygga långsiktiga relationer för framgångsrika affärer. Du är proaktiv och trivs med att skapa nya kontakter som gör skillnad i vardagen.


Vi ser även att du:

* Har goda kunskaper i Excel och gärna SharePoint
* Trivs i en administrativ roll och är skicklig i skriftlig kommunikation
* Har erfarenhet av liknande roll (meriterande)
* Har ett intresse för träning och hälsa – det är ett stort plus

**Vad vi erbjuder dig**
-----------------------

* En central roll där du får kombinera struktur, service och affär
* En arbetsplats med hög energi, träningsglädje och stark gemenskap
* Möjlighet att utvecklas inom försäljning och affärsstöd
* Kollegor som stöttar och inspirerar
* Ett företag som växer – där du kan växa med oss

**Placering:** Göteborg, Huvudkontoret Backaplan alt annan ort  

**Anställningsform:** Heltid, Tillsvidare med 6 månaders provanställning  

**Start:** Enligt överenskommelse

**Sök redan idag!**
-------------------


Om du går vidare till slutskedet av rekryteringsprocessen kan en bakgrundskontroll komma att genomföras. Detta sker alltid med ditt samtycke.


För oss på Nordic Wellness är en trygg och hälsosam arbetsmiljö viktigt. Som en del av vårt förebyggande arbetsmiljöarbete tillämpar vi slumpmässiga drogtester.


Urval och intervjuer sker löpande – så vänta inte. Skicka in din ansökan idag och bli en del av Nordic Wellness-familjen, där vi tillsammans lever våra värdeord:  

**FUN MOVEMENT STRONG TOGETHER**
company_description: Alla har rätt till ett riktigt bra gym – oavsett vem du är, var du bor eller varför du tränar. Med över 300 klubbar från Luleå till Ystad erbjuder vi därför det senaste inom träning och hälsa.

title: Embedded Security Project Manager - Nordics
description:
**Embedded Security Project Manager - Nordics**

**Physical & Electronic Security - Global Clients**

* **Location: EMEA-based, flexible (Sweden, Denmark, Norway, Finland)**
* **Travel: Regular site travel required, typically every 1–2 weeks, depending on regional project demand.**

**Purpose**


This role strengthens delivery capability across large‑scale data centre and critical infrastructure programmes for a major global technology customer. Operating as an embedded technical specialist, the Security Project Manager leads the on‑site coordination, technical validation, and integrated deployment of physical and electronic security systems.


The role blends project delivery, engineering oversight, on‑site presence, and system verification not traditional commercial project management. You will support in‑country project managers and partner with construction, MEP, network, and global security teams to ensure systems are installed, tested, and handed over to the highest standards.

**Major Responsibilities**

**Technical Security Delivery**

* Deliver the full lifecycle of physical and electronic security elements within data centre and critical infrastructure projects.
* Oversee technical installation quality, system integration accuracy, device placement, panel wiring, and alignment to global standards.
* Support site surveys, verify existing conditions, and collaborate with local teams to scope and document technical requirements.

**On‑Site Coordination & Field Presence**

* Serve as the on‑site embedded security representative, collaborating closely with construction, MEP, and infrastructure teams.
* Conduct regular on‑site reviews to validate installation progress, sequencing, and technical compliance.
* Engage directly with integrators and contractors to resolve issues and accelerate progress.

**Functional Testing & System Verification**

* Lead functional testing of ACS, VMS, IDS, intercoms, sensors, physical hardware, and failover behaviour.
* Verify door functionality (REX, DPS), camera behaviour, alarm routing, network logic paths, redundancy, and event visibility.
* Document results, identify defects, and coordinate remediation through re‑testing and sign‑off.

**Commissioning Support (Verification, Not Programming)**

* Witness and validate commissioning performed by integrators.
* Verify system responses, alarms, behaviour, and integration points, ensuring readiness for handover.
* Confirm alignment to global client standards and escalate deviations where required.

**Physical Security Infrastructure Oversight**

* Oversee installation and integration of:
* Perimeter fencing and hardening
* Turnstiles, gates, vehicle barriers, crash‑rated bollards
* Door hardware, locking systems, hinges, closers
* Ensure seamless interplay between physical components and electronic systems.

**Documentation, Reporting & Quality Management**

* Produce structured site reports with photos, risks, deviations, and recommended corrective actions.
* Track and close snags/punch lists, coordinating remediation with in‑country partners.
* Review RFIs, submittals, drawings, and technical documentation to support accurate delivery.

**Design, BIM & Coordination**

* Review drawings and models (Bluebeam, ACC, Navisworks) to identify clashes, design gaps, access constraints, and installation conflicts.
* Provide redlines and recommendations that improve buildability and prevent rework.

**Cross‑Functional Partnership**

* Partner with global security standards owners, engineering, construction, MEP, and operations teams.
* Maintain clear communication through dashboards, updates, and issue/risk registers.
* Align project activities with global security expectations while adapting to local conditions.

**What Does Success look like**

* You enhance the capacity of regional project managers by delivering technically accurate, fully validated security systems.
* You ensure installations are correct the first time, reducing rework, delays, and escalations.
* You create clarity for all stakeholders through structured, proactive communication.
* You build trusted relationships across construction, engineering, and security teams.
* You help the global programme scale consistently, supporting expansion across multiple regions.
* You champion continuous improvement by identifying gaps, improving testing processes, and elevating delivery quality.

**Education**

* Qualification in engineering, security technology, construction management, or related field; equivalent experience considered.

**Experience & Knowledge**

**Technical Security Experience**

* Strong practical experience with **Lenel OnGuard** and **Milestone XProtect** (essential).
* Familiarity with ACS/VMS integrations, intrusion detection, intercoms, sensors, IP‑based security systems.
* Experience validating functional tests and witnessing commissioning activities.
* Understanding of installation methods (cabling, containment, device mounting, infrastructure routing).

**Physical Security Infrastructure**

* Experience with perimeter systems, gates, barriers, turnstiles, and door hardware.
* Knowledge of how physical and electronic systems integrate logically and mechanically.

**Critical Infrastructure Delivery**

* Experience delivering security systems in data centres, utilities, energy, telecoms, transport hubs, or similar high‑availability environments.

**Tools & Platforms**

* Bluebeam Revu
* Autodesk Construction Cloud (ACC)
* Navisworks (Manage or Freedom)
* MS Project or Smartsheet
* AutoCAD
* Microsoft 365
* BIM/VDC familiarity beneficial

**Skills and Competencies**

* Collaborative, relationship‑focused approach
* Strong technical problem‑solving capability
* Clear, confident communication across diverse stakeholders
* High attention to detail, structured working style
* Ability to work independently and travel regularly
* Adaptability in fast‑moving construction environments
* Commitment to continuous learning and technical growth

**Why Join Us?**

* Contribute to the delivery of some of the world’s most advanced data centre and critical infrastructure environments.
* Work embedded with a global technology giant on high‑impact, long‑term programmes.
* Be part of a supportive, high‑performing engineering and programme delivery community.
* Access professional development, certifications (Lenel, Milestone, VMS/ACS), and future progression opportunities.
* Enjoy flexibility, trust, and a culture that values inclusion, technical mastery, and real impact.

**Our People Promise – Securitas Technology EVP**


Join a values-driven, diverse, and inclusive workplace. We offer clear career pathways, growth opportunities, recognition for achievements, and a global, collaborative team. You’ll do purposeful work that makes the world safer, with support for innovation and sustainability.


As part of our EVP, we offer:

* Career pathways and growth opportunities
* A global, values-driven team
* Recognition for achievements
* Inclusive, collaborative culture
* Purposeful work that makes the world safer
* Support for innovation and sustainability


Securitas Technology is committed to diversity and is an equal opportunity employer; all qualified applicants are welcome.
company_description: Securitas’ mission is to protect homes, workplaces, and communities by providing the security services they need to protect their assets and safeguard their people.

title: Record to Report Lead
description:
*At Aceve, we empower the builders of society and transform the construction and skilled trades industry—one of the least digitalized sectors in Europe. As a united SaaS powerhouse, we bring together leading products into a single pioneering platform dedicated to making a difference.*  

  

*With over 900 passionate employees and more than €100 million in revenue, we blend tradition and innovation to reshape our industry’s future. Our cloud-based, AI-driven software solutions deliver actionable insights and drive improvements for construction companies and skilled tradespeople, enhancing project management, installations, maintenance, and services.*  

  

*On our ambitious growth journey, we are committed to winning together and accelerating digital transformation. With Aceve’s tools, our customers can boost their performance, productivity, and profitability, ensuring they thrive in a rapidly changing landscape.*  

  

*Join us in revolutionizing the industry and building a brighter future together!*  

  

**About the role**


We are looking for an experienced and people-driven R2R Lead to join our SSC leadership team. This is a pivotal role for Aceve's finance operations — leading the Record-to-Report team responsible for high-quality, timely, and compliant financial reporting across a growing international footprint. This role is ideal for a hands-on leader who thrives in a fast-moving, high-growth, and M&A-rich environment — someone who can build structure where needed while empowering people to grow.

**What will you do**


You'll lead and develop a talented team of controllers, driving harmonization, automation, and continuous improvement across all entities — working closely with the SSC Leadership team and the Group Finance and FP&A teams.

* Lead and mentor the R2R team within the SSC, fostering ownership, development, and collaboration.
* Ensure accurate, compliant, and timely financial reporting across multiple entities in line with IFRS and local GAAP.
* Drive standardization and process excellence in month-end closings, reconciliations, and intercompany accounting.
* Act as key finance integration support for new acquisitions — onboarding entities into the SSC framework, tools, and controls.
* Partner closely with Group Finance and FP&A to align end-to-end processes from source to report.
* Identify and execute on automation and digitalization opportunities to enhance efficiency and quality.
* Collaborate with external auditors and advisors to secure statutory compliance and smooth audits.
* Own and evolve R2R KPIs and quality metrics, ensuring continuous improvement and transparency.
* Build a strong culture of accountability, teamwork, and learning within the SSC.

**We believe you have:**

* 7+ years of experience in accounting and controlling, ideally in a multi-entity or M&A-driven environment.
* 3+ years of leadership experience, ideally in a Shared Service or process-driven environment.
* A degree in Finance, Accounting, or Economics.
* Strong knowledge of accounting and reporting frameworks, including IFRS, K3, and local GAAP.
* Solid ERP experience (preferably NetSuite) and proficiency in Excel and Power BI.
* Proven people development skills — with a track record of building and motivating high-performing teams.
* A structured yet pragmatic mindset, balancing accuracy with agility.
* Strong communication skills in English — this is a requirement.
* Energy, curiosity, and resilience — you thrive in change and take pride in making complexity simple.

**Bonus Points For:**

* Prior experience leading finance integrations in an M&A-intensive environment.
* Familiarity with automation or digitalization tools in a finance operations context.

 **Not ticking every box?** No worries! We value potential and drive, so apply anyway or connect with us- you might be just what we're looking for in our growing organisation! Join Aceve and take your expertise to the next level, together we are redefining what’s possible in craft and construction solutions!

 *Aceve is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. Our selection process is conducted continuously and without discrimination, ensuring an equal opportunity for all candidates.*

title: Enterprise Architect
description:
The key word is **change**. Technology is no longer a support function. Agents are becoming active participants in workflows. New entrants are reshaping the SaaS landscape and turning multi-year contracts into strategic liabilities. Governance demands around data sovereignty, security, and responsible use of AI are expanding. And velocity mismatches between strategy, architecture, and governance remain the largest source of wasted IT investment today.


We are seeking an **Enterprise Architect** to design and govern the holistic blueprint that connects business strategy, applications, data, and technology across our clients’ organisations. Someone who can see the whole picture and make it coherent.

**Who is Umain?**
-----------------


We were founded with a **heritage in digital product development and engineering**. This background gives us a unique perspective, ensuring that our strategic advice is always grounded in **technical feasibility and execution excellence**. We are now expanding our focus to help clients at the C-level, linking core technology strategy directly to business outcomes.


Umain is proudly part of **Eidra**, a consultancy collective driven by the conviction that business transformation requires a combination of management consulting and best-in-breed expertise in creativity, engineering, and data. This partnership means that while you focus on strategic IT-Management and enterprise architecture, you have full access to orchestrate common capabilities from the best specialized companies within the collective.


Our team consists of individuals with experience from the largest consulting firms, but with the flexibility, speed, and entrepreneurial drive that characterize a modern, flat organization. At Umain, we highly value **collaboration** and **entrepreneurial spirit**. Here, you are not only expected to lead demanding client assignments; you are also expected to actively contribute to our internal development, whether it involves shaping new service offerings in AI/Data or mentoring our junior talent. We reward those who take ownership and drive us forward.

**The Role: What will you do?**
-------------------------------


As an **Enterprise Architect** within Eidra’s Tech Advisory & IT Management practice, you are the structural backbone of our client engagements. You work across assessment, design, and transformation, ensuring that architectural decisions hold up from boardroom to codebase. Your assignments will involve:

* **Architecture Assessment & Scoring:** Evaluating client technology estates for maturity, composability, technical debt, and swap-readiness. Producing actionable findings that connect directly to business outcomes and sourcing decisions.
* **Enterprise Architecture Design:** Designing composable, API-first architectures that replace rigid target-state blueprints with modular, swap-ready designs. Guiding major technology decisions on platforms, integration patterns, and cloud strategy.
* **AI Readiness & Data Architecture**: Assessing and designing the data and platform foundations needed for AI adoption. Designing model-agnostic AI layers that enable provider flexibility and reduce lock-in.
* **Strategic Communication**: Translating architecture decisions into business impact language for CIOs and boards. Connecting architecture findings to investment priorities, sourcing strategy, and risk posture.
* **Internal Leadership**: Actively contributing to Eidra’s business development, mentoring colleagues, and developing our architecture competency pool.

**Who are you?**
----------------


We are looking for someone who:

* Has **7+ years of relevant experience** in enterprise architecture, technology strategy, or IT transformation. Typical backgrounds include **Enterprise Architect, Solution Architect, CTO, or Technology Advisor**.
* Is driven by a deep passion for making complex technology landscapes **coherent, composable, and aligned** with business strategy.
* Has a documented ability to **assess, design, and govern enterprise architecture** across diverse industries and technology estates.
* Has a natural ability to **structure and communicate complex architectural concepts** to both engineering teams and C-suite stakeholders.

**Required Competencies**
-------------------------

* **Enterprise Architecture Practice**: Deep expertise in enterprise architecture frameworks adapted for modern, AI-era contexts. Experience maintaining architectural principles as standing policies and governing technology decisions across the enterprise.
* **Platform & Integration Design**: Hands-on experience with enterprise platforms and business-critical systems (e.g. ERP, CRM, modern data stacks), combined with strong expertise in integration architecture, API strategy, and cloud architecture.
* **Composable Architecture**: Ability to design modular, API-first architectures and assess technology components for swap-readiness, lock-in risk, and technical debt.
* **Strategic AI**: Solid understanding of how to design AI-ready platforms, including model-agnostic AI layers, data architecture for AI/ML workloads, and the implications of AI on enterprise architecture.
* **Business-Technology Alignment**: A proven ability to connect architecture decisions to business outcomes, investment cases, and sourcing strategy.
* **Collaborative Mindset**: A collaborative and open approach, with a genuine commitment to continuous development, team building, and long-term client relationships.
* **Language:** Fluency in English, both written and spoken, is essential.


Do you want to help build the future digital backbone for our clients?

 ***Background check***

*Umain conducts a background check for final candidates in accordance with our internal policies. The check is carried out only with your consent and is ordered by our CISO through our approved screening partner. The scope of the check depends on the role level. This step is performed late in the recruitment process and is used solely to support a secure and fact‑based hiring decision.*

title: Technology Management Consultant
description:
The key word is change. In a world where technology and business are merging, we are seeking a Technology Management Consultant to plan, lead, and execute IT projects and transformation initiatives, taking ownership of delivery from scoping through to implementation across business applications and technical platforms.

**Who is Umain?**
-----------------


We were founded with a **heritage in digital product development and engineering**. This background gives us a unique perspective, ensuring that our strategic advice is always grounded in **technical feasibility and execution excellence**. Umain is proudly part of **Eidra**, a consultancy collective driven by the conviction that business transformation requires a combination of management consulting and best-in-breed expertise in creativity, engineering, and data. Our team consists of individuals with experience from the largest consulting firms, but with the flexibility, speed, and entrepreneurial drive that characterize a modern, flat organization. At Umain, we highly value **collaboration** and **entrepreneurial spirit**. Here, you are not only expected to lead demanding client assignments; you are also expected to actively contribute to our internal development, whether it involves shaping new service offerings in AI/Data or mentoring our junior talent. We reward those who take ownership and drive us forward.

**The Role: What will you do?**
-------------------------------


As a Technology Management Consultant within Tech Advisory & IT Management, you work at the intersection of business and technology, helping clients structure and execute their IT initiatives. Your assignments will involve:

* **IT Strategy & Enterprise Architecture:** Supporting clients in technology direction, investment priorities, and portfolio decisions. Helping translate business goals into future-state architecture for business-critical applications.
* **Governance, Operating Model & Sourcing:** Helping clients design the structures that make technology delivery work, from IT governance frameworks and risk management to operating model design, vendor strategy, and FinOps.
* **Project & Program Management:** Leading and coordinating IT projects and workstreams, managing teams, tracking delivery, and ensuring strategic intent translates into execution reality.
* **Internal Contribution:** Actively contributing to Eidra's business development, mentoring junior colleagues, and developing our competency pool.

**Who are you?**
----------------


We are looking for someone who:

* Has **3+ years of relevant experience**, preferably within a consultancy setting, across IT project management or technology transformation, in roles such as I**T Consultant, Project Manager, Business Analyst, or Solution Architect.**
* Is driven by a passion for translating technology strategy into **measurable business outcomes**.
* Has experience **leading and coordinating IT initiatives** and cross-functional project teams.
* Is a collaborative team player who **creates structure, clarity, and momentum** in delivery.
* **Communicates** complex technical concepts **clearly** to both technical teams and business stakeholders.

**Required Competencies**
-------------------------

* **IT Domain Knowledge:** Working knowledge across at least two of Eidra's five IT management domains: IT Strategy & Portfolio, Enterprise Architecture & Data, Operating Model & Workforce, Governance Risk & Trust, or Sourcing & FinOps.
* **System & Architecture Experience:** Hands-on experience with enterprise platforms and business-critical systems (e.g. ERP, CRM, modern data stacks, proprietary solutions), combined with a foundational understanding of integration patterns and cloud architecture.
* **Strategic AI:** A solid understanding of how to utilize and integrate AI tools and technologies in core business processes and systems.
* **Collaborative Mindset:** A collaborative and open approach, with a genuine commitment to continuous development, team building, and long-term client relationships.
* **Analytical Drive:** A critical-thinking mindset with the ability to identify bottlenecks and opportunities, and translate complexity into clear, actionable recommendations.
* **Language:** Fluency in English, both written and spoken, is essential.

***Background check***

*Umain conducts a background check for final candidates in accordance with our* internal policies*. The check is carried out only with your consent and is ordered by our CISO through our approved screening partner. The scope of the check depends on the role level. This step is performed late in the recruitment process and is used solely to support a secure and fact‑based hiring decision.*
