title: Linux & Plattformsspecialist (Kubernetes / IBM-miljö)
description:
Nion är ett IT-konsultbolag med kontor i Stockholm, Malmö, Göteborg, Skopje, Bitola och Sofia, där över 200 IT-specialister arbetar tillsammans för att leverera skräddarsydda digitala lösningar. Våra kunder verkar inom bland annat bank och finans, offentlig sektor, telekom, fintech och försäkringar – och vi hjälper organisationer att förenkla komplexa utmaningar och skapa verkligt värde.


Just nu söker vi en erfaren och tekniskt driven Linux- och plattformsspecialist som vill arbeta i en avancerad IT-miljö med fokus på containerplattformar, databaser och infrastruktur.


I rollen kommer du arbeta nära både kund och team för att säkerställa stabil drift och vidareutveckling av plattformar baserade på Linux och Kubernetes/OpenShift. Du bidrar med din expertis inom systemadministration, nätverk och databaser, samt stöttar i tekniska vägval kopplade till plattform, säkerhet och licenshantering.

**Om rollen**


I denna roll arbetar du med drift, förvaltning och utveckling av en komplex IT-miljö där stabilitet, säkerhet och prestanda är centralt. Du blir en nyckelperson i att säkerställa att plattformar och system fungerar effektivt och hållbart över tid.

**Kravprofil**


Vi söker dig som har goda kunskaper inom:

* Minst 5 års arbetslivserfarenhet av Linux-administration
* Minst 2 års arbetslivserfarenhet av Databaser, särskilt Db2
* Minst 1 års arbetslivserfarenhet av Nätverk
* Erfarenhet av Kubernetes / OpenShift

 **Meriterande erfarenheter**


Det är extra positivt om du även har erfarenhet av:

* IBM Cloud Pak
* HCL BigFix
* IBM License Metric Tool (ILMT)
* IBM License Service för Kubernetes
* Generell kunskap om IBM-licensmodeller
* Tidigare arbete som konsult

 **Övriga krav**

* Du behöver kunna genomgå och bli godkänd i säkerhetsprövning
* Du är villig att arbeta på plats i Enköping cirka 2 dagar per vecka
* Relevant arbetslivserfarenhet
* Relevant Högskoleutbildning eller motsvarande utbildning inom YH
* Flytande i svenska och engelska – skrift och tal

 **Vem är du?**


Vi tror att du är en lösningsorienterad och självgående person som trivs i tekniskt komplexa miljöer. Du har ett strukturerat arbetssätt och ett stort intresse för drift, plattformar och modern infrastruktur.  

  

**Hos oss får du**

* En samarbetsinriktad och platt organisation där stöd är en självklarhet. Din konsultchef fungerar som mentor för att säkerställa din professionella utveckling och trivsel.
* En tydlig karriärplan och kontinuerliga möjligheter till lärande genom projekt, mentorskap och kompetensutveckling.
* En levande företagskultur med stark teamkänsla, kunskapsdelning och sociala aktiviteter som gör jobbet roligt.

 **Utvalda förmåner**

* Möjlighet till delägarskap
* Konkurrenskraftig fast lön
* 30 dagar semester
* Företagsdator, mobil och abonnemang.

  

Redo för nästa steg i karriären? Skicka in din ansökan till Nion

title: Systemkonsult
description:
****Systemkonsult: Azure & AI-driven utveckling****
 Vill du bygga framtidens molnlösningar och driva utveckling med AI?
   

  

 Vi söker nu en systemkonsult för att stärka vår tillväxtresa. I denna roll kommer du att vara en del av ett innovativt SaaS-bolag där din kompetens inom cloudlösningar och Azure Integration Services blir direkt avgörande för att optimera våra kunders verksamhet genom sömlösa och moderna integrationer
   

  

****Rollen****
 Som systemkonsult är du bryggan mellan teknik och kundnytta. Du kommer att ha en central roll i utvecklingen och leveransen av vårt affärssystem. Du arbetar nära kunder för att förstå deras behov och designa lösningar som effektiviserar deras verksamheter. Vi befinner oss i ett spännande skede där vi flyttar våra produkter till molnet, och ditt fokus är att säkerställa sömlösa integrationer för våra kunder. I din vardag kommer de huvudsakliga uppgifterna att innebära:
   

  

* Designa och bygga nya integrationer från grunden.
* Ta ett helhetsansvar för integrationsprojekt, från arkitektur till stabil leverans, löpande förvaltning och optimering.
* Anpassa rapporter och göra konverteringar åt kund.
* Ta del av relevanta Azure-certifieringar för att ligga i framkant.


****Tech Stack****
* Azure Integration Services
* C#, .NET
* REST-API:er.
* MS SQL Server (T-SQL, SSIS).


****Vem är du?****
 Vi söker en självgående lagspelare med erfarenhet av systemutveckling och molnintegrationer. Du tar naturligt helhetsansvar, är nyfiken på ny teknik och vill att din utveckling ska drivas av AI. Du trivs i en roll som kombinerar ett djupt tekniskt fokus med ett genuint intresse för kundens affärsnytta. I din roll kommer du ha ett nära samarbete med kollegor inom olika kompetensområden för att säkerställa att vi levererar högkvalitativa lösningar.
   

  

 Vi välkomnar även dig som är nyexaminerad med en stark teknisk grund och ett brinnande intresse för att utvecklas inom Azure och AI.
   

  

 Du är affärsinriktad, strukturerad och kommunicerar väl på svenska och engelska.
   

  

****Produkten****
 Du arbetar med Control Edge – Vismas moderna, molnbaserade ekonomisystem.
   

  

* Byggt för komplexa organisationer
* Fokus på AI, automation och säkerhet
* Höga krav på prestanda och skalbarhet


****Varför Visma Software Nordic?****
 På Visma Software Nordic blir du en del av en kompetent och driven organisation. Vi verkar i en snabbrörlig bransch och är ständigt på tårna för att utmana befintliga arbetssätt i syfte att ligga steget före våra konkurrenter. Du kommer att ingå i en stark professionell miljö med fokus på innovation, samarbete och kontinuerlig utveckling — samtidigt som du arbetar i kärnan av vår tillväxtresa. Vi erbjuder konkurrenskraftiga villkor, stora utvecklingsmöjligheter och chansen att påverka
   

  

**Malmö eller Stockholm | Hybrid**
****Ansökan****
 Vi tillämpar löpande urval. Personligt brev undanbedes – bifoga CV eller LinkedIn-profil.
   

  

 Kontakt: Åsa Duvander, Consultant Manager | asa.duvander@visma.com

title: Lösningsarkitekt Kafka
description:
**Assignment Description**
 Till en större svensk myndighet inom samhällskritisk verksamhet söker vi en konsult till uppdraget nedan:
   

  

**Uppdragsbeskrivning:**
 Uppdraget innebär att bidra till implementering och förvaltning av en Kafka-plattform med höga krav på tillgänglighet och säkerhet i en lokal (on prem) miljö.
   

  

 Vidare ingår att bygga och underhålla dataflöden som integrerar en bred variation av databaser, API:er och olika protokoll.
   

  

 Arbetet omfattar även löpande förvaltning, övervakning och felsökning av lösningarna för att säkerställa stabil drift och kontinuerlig förbättring.
   

  

 Rollen innebär ett nära samarbete med kollegor inom den egna gruppen samt angränsande team, där konsulten förväntas agera tekniskt bollplank och aktivt bidra till kunskapsspridning kring lösningar och teknik.
   

  

**Kvalificeringskrav (Skallkrav):**
* Konsulten ska ha god förmåga att uttrycka sig i tal och skrift på svenska.
* Minst 5 års erfarenhet som lösningsarkitekt.
* Dokumenterad erfarenhet av flera uppdrag att sätta upp och administrera Apache Kafka i on-premise miljöer med fokus på hög tillgänglighet och säkerhet.
* Praktisk erfarenhet av loggning, monitorering och certifikatshantering.
* Dokumenterad erfarenhet av GitOps och infrastruktur som kod.
* Dokumenterad erfarenhet av containerteknologi och orkestrering lokalt.
* Dokumenterad erfarenhet av Kafka Connect samt konfigurering av konnektorer mot olika databaser, API:er och protokoll.
* Erfarenhet av att implementera egenutvecklade komponenter i Kafka.
* Erfarenhet av att designa Kafka-arkitektur för horisontell skalning
* Erfarenhet av stream processing

title: Endpoint Management Specialist till Sweco!
description:
Har du erfarenhet av enhetshantering i större miljöer och vill ta nästa steg i karriären? Är du tekniskt nyfiken, samarbetsorienterad och gillar att lösa problem? Då kan rollen som
 **Endpoint Management Specialist hos Sweco** 
 vara rätt för dig.
   

  

**Om rollen**
 Som Endpoint Management Specialist blir du en del av
 **AD & Endpoint services** 
 teamet på åtta personer inom Infrastructure & Cloud. Du samarbetar tätt med flera andra team och har ett helhetsansvar för hanteringen av tusentals användarklienter – exempelvis datorer, mobila enheter och de system som stödjer dem.
   

  

 I rollen kommer du att vara en viktig del i den pågående förflyttningen från en traditionell on-prem-miljö med SCCM till en modern, molnbaserad endpoint-plattform där Intune är navet. Arbetet omfattar både migrering av PC-klienter och mobila enheter. Parallellt med migreringsarbetet ansvarar du för att drifta och vidareutveckla endpoint-miljön, hantera nya behov och förfrågningar från verksamheten samt utvärdera och implementera nya funktioner och förbättringar.
   

  

**Vem är du?**
 För att lyckas i rollen ser vi att du har några års erfarenhet av liknande arbete och har jobbat med endpoint management i större Intune i molnet. Du behärskar svenska och engelska flytande i tal och skrift.
   

  

**Vi Söker Dig Som Har**
* Arbetslivserfarenhet av Intune i större miljö
* Arbetslivserfarenhet av Powershell för script och automation
* Arbetslivserfarenhet av Windows
* Arbetslivserfarenhet av att managera mobila enheter
* Arbetslivserfarenhet av Azure


**Vi Ser Det Som Meriterande Om Du Har**
* Erfarenhet av virtuella klientmiljöer
* Erfarenhet av Mac
* Erfarenhet av SCCM


 Som person är du tekniskt intresserad, nyfiken och motiverad att utvecklas. Du trivs med att arbeta i team, är lyhörd för andras behov och har ett lösningsorienterat mind set. Du tar gärna ansvar, driver arbetet framåt och kommunicerar tydligt med din omgivning.
   

  

**Ansökan**
*I denna rekrytering samarbetar Sweco* 

*med Wise.
   

  

 Vi tar inte emot ansökningar via mail, utan du söker enkelt med ditt CV eller din LinkedIn-profil på* 
***wise.se***
*. Vi använder kompetensbaserad rekrytering som metod. Det gör vi i vår strävan efter en mer potentialbaserad matchning. Vi ser därför gärna sökanden med olika bakgrunder, åldrar och erfarenheter, eftersom vi vet att en bredare mångfald berikar var och en av oss och gör oss bättre och smartare som organisation.
   

  

 Har du frågor om processen eller rollen? Kontakta då ansvarig rekryteringskonsult Jacob Elmeljung på* 
*jacob.elmeljung@wise.se* 
***.***
*Varmt välkommen med din ansökan!*
**Om Sweco**
 Transforming society together, we’re planning and designing sustainable communities and cities of the future.
   

  

 Solving the great societal challenges of our time is more relevant, demanding and inspiring than ever before. At Sweco, we bring together experts with different perspectives and skills to develop solutions that address the challenges of urbanisation, harness the positive power of digitalisation and make societies more sustainable.
   

  

 We offer our clients a combination of global expertise and local presence, adapted to their business and reality. Our ambition is to be our clients’ most relevant partner, working together to help them solve their challenges, today and into the future.
   

  

 Sweco is committed to enabling all its colleagues to achieve a great balance between work and their other priorities, such as caring responsibilities, leisure activities, further learning or other interests. We offer flexible start and finish times, part time and hybrid working options so that you can flex your hours around your day to day needs.

title: Solutions Engineer
description:
FEQ327R96
   

  

 At Databricks, we help organizations unify their data, analytics, and AI on one platform. Our mission is to simplify how teams work with data and accelerate innovation. As a Solutions Engineer, you’ll partner with customers to solve real-world data challenges by combining technical expertise with business context to demonstrate what’s possible on the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform. You’ll join through our Launchpad program, a structured onboarding experience designed to help you ramp quickly and build confidence in customer-facing work.
   

  

**The Impact You Will Have**
* Help customers realize the value of the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform as a trusted technical partner to account teams
* Progress through FE Launchpad from structured learning and shadowing to independently leading customer engagements
* Build demos and prototypes across data engineering, analytics, and AI/GenAI to solve real customer problems
* Partner with Account Executives, Solutions Architects, and cross-functional teams to shape customer architectures and drive lakehouse and Lakebase adoption
* Grow through certifications, bootcamps, and mentorship, and share your learning with your cohort and the broader FE community


**What We Look For**
* Early-career professionals with ~2 years of experience in data engineering, software engineering, AI/ML, or a related field who are interested in customer-facing technical roles.
* A strong technical foundation (STEM degree or equivalent experience) and curiosity about modern data architectures, AI applications, and real-time analytics
* Interest in working with customers; running discovery, understanding challenges, and explaining technical concepts clearly to both technical and business audiences
* Clear communication skills, strong collaboration, and a growth mindset suited to a cohort-based learning environment
* Ownership mindset; you take initiative, build, and iterate on ideas (e.g., demos, apps, or MVPs)
* Foundational familiarity with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP
* Exposure to Databricks or cloud certifications


**About Databricks**
 Databricks is the data and AI company. More than 10,000 organizations worldwide — including Comcast, Condé Nast, Grammarly, and over 50% of the Fortune 500 — rely on the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform to unify and democratize data, analytics and AI. Databricks is headquartered in San Francisco, with offices around the globe and was founded by the original creators of Lakehouse, Apache Spark™, Delta Lake and MLflow. To learn more, follow Databricks on Twitter, LinkedIn and Facebook.
   

  

**Benefits**
 At Databricks, we strive to provide comprehensive benefits and perks that meet the needs of all of our employees. For specific details on the benefits offered in your region, please visit https://www.mybenefitsnow.com/databricks.
   

  

**Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion**
 At Databricks, we are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive culture where everyone can excel. We take great care to ensure that our hiring practices are inclusive and meet equal employment opportunity standards. Individuals looking for employment at Databricks are considered without regard to age, color, disability, ethnicity, family or marital status, gender identity or expression, language, national origin, physical and mental ability, political affiliation, race, religion, sexual orientation, socio-economic status, veteran status, and other protected characteristics.
   

  

**Compliance**
**If access to export-controlled technology or source code is required for performance of job duties, it is within Employer's discretion whether to apply for a U.S. government license for such positions, and Employer may decline to proceed with an applicant on this basis alone.**

title: Enterprise Business Development Manager
description:
****Job Req ID: 27999****
**About Supermicro**
 Supermicro® is a Top Tier provider of advanced server, storage, and networking solutions for Data Center, Cloud Computing, Enterprise IT, Hadoop/ Big Data, Hyperscale, HPC and IoT/Embedded customers worldwide. We are amongst the fastest growing company among the Silicon Valley Top 50 technology firms. Our unprecedented global expansion has provided us with the opportunity to offer many new positions to the technology community. We seek talented, passionate, and committed engineers, technologists, and business leaders to join us to drive accelerated growth and technology-led innovation in the global marketplace.
   

  

**Job Summary**
 Supermicro is seeking an experienced Business Development Manager to join our EMEA team. This role is focused on winning new enterprise customers and expanding our footprint within existing accounts. You will drive revenue growth by promoting Supermicro’s broad solutions portfolio, building multi-level executive relationships, and orchestrating cross-functional alignment to close high-value opportunities. The ideal candidate brings a proven track record in business development, a customer-first mindset, and the ability to translate strategy into measurable results across the EMEA region.
   

  

**Essential Duties And Responsibilities**
* Identify, win and expand enterprise customer accounts across AI training/inference, HPC, data platforms, cloud, IoT and edge.
* Build multi-threaded relationships and executive sponsorship in large complex customers.
* Drive joint pursuits with key industry alliances and technology partners.
* Maintain and grow relationships with existing customers by identifying cross-sell/upsell paths to increase share of wallet.
* Research trends and market signals to open new sales areas and craft vertical plays (Finance, Public Sector, Telco/Media, Manufacturing, Retail, Energy).
* Operate with discipline: keep pipeline accurate and current, progress deals with clear next actions, and forecast credibly.
* Collaborate cross-functionally with commercial, technical and operations teams to ensure customer experience excellence.


**Qualifications**
* 5-10+ years in enterprise business development / strategic sales for infrastructure (servers, storage, AI/HPC, cloud/edge) with a record of new-logo wins and design-in selling.
* Proven ability to originate pipeline and manage complex, multi-stakeholder pursuits (IT, LoB, procurement, partners).
* Proven track record of driving enterprise business development and sales across the EMEA region.
* Ability to translate technical concepts into vertical-aligned value propositions, linking workload/architecture decisions to measurable outcomes in executive discussions.
* Strong process rigour with excellent pipeline hygiene, crisp written/oral communications and executive presence.


****EEO Statement****
 Supermicro is an Equal Opportunity Employer and embraces diversity in our employee population. It is the policy of Supermicro to provide equal opportunity to all qualified applicants and employees without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, protected veteran status or special disabled veteran, marital status, pregnancy, genetic information, or any other legally protected status.

title: Senior Account Manager
description:
\*This vacancy can be located from our offices at either Rijswijk (Netherlands), Zaventem (Belgium) or Stockholm (Sweden)\*
   

  

* The role will be published until 10th April 2026\*


 As a Senior Account Manager, become the essential bridge between TUI's regional marketing teams and our in-house creative studio, TUI Studios. In this key role, you'll translate business objectives into compelling creative campaigns while ensuring brand consistency and driving creative excellence across your aligned region.
   

  

**About Our Offer**
* Personal benefits: Attractive remuneration, discretionary bonus schemes, generous travel benefits, extensive health & well-being support, and more.
* Flexible working: Work is something you do, not somewhere you go. We encourage a healthy work-life balance with a dynamic working environment.
* A career to shape: Access the TUI Learning Hub to level-up and reach your ambitions.
* Broaden your network: We champion intercultural collaboration and provide opportunities to work on global projects and teams.
* Community: Get involved with incredible local charity and sustainability initiatives like the TUI Care Foundation.


**About The Job**
* You'll act as the primary liaison between internal clients and the creative studio, building strong relationships with senior stakeholders while translating business objectives into actionable creative briefs.
* Overseeing the end-to-end delivery of creative and product activity will be central to your role, ensuring timelines, budgets, and quality standards are consistently met.
* Working closely with the Creative Lead, you'll champion creative excellence while balancing commercial and operational realities across all deliverables.
* We'll rely on you to coordinate cross-functional teams including creative, production, design, copy, publishing, and localization to deliver cohesive campaign assets.
* With your regional knowledge, you'll oversee transcreation and translation in conjunction with the Brand Lead and Localization Lead.
* You'll participate in strategic discussions around localization, brand guardianship, and content governance, helping to evolve our in-house creative studio model.


**About You**
* You bring experience working in creative, content, advertising, or marketing, with proven success in an in-house creative studio or marketing agency in a similar account management role.
* You have developed strong project management abilities that allow you to handle multiple stakeholders and projects simultaneously.
* You can translate business objectives into creative briefs effectively, balancing creative excellence with commercial priorities.
* You have an organized approach to work with attention to detail and can deliver under pressure while meeting deadlines.
* You communicate effectively and build productive relationships across different departments and levels of seniority.
* You understand creative processes and production workflows, with experience in the travel industry and multi-regional environments being advantageous.


 From a workplace to a place to belong. At TUI we embrace diversity, equity, and inclusion, encouraging everyone to come as you are, because together, our potential is limitless.
   

  

 We are committed to supporting candidates with disabilities and impairments so if you require any support, please do let us know.

title: Staff Backend Engineer - Adaptive Telemetry | Sweden | Remote
description:
Grafana Labs is a remote-first, open-source powerhouse. There are more than 20M users of Grafana, the open source visualization tool, around the globe, monitoring everything from beehives to climate change in the Alps. The instantly recognizable dashboards have been spotted everywhere from a NASA launch and Minecraft HQ to Wimbledon and the Tour de France. Grafana Labs also helps more than 3,000 companies -- including Bloomberg, JPMorgan Chase, and eBay -- manage their observability strategies with the Grafana LGTM Stack, which can be run fully managed with Grafana Cloud or self-managed with the Grafana Enterprise Stack, both featuring scalable metrics (Grafana Mimir), logs (Grafana Loki), and traces (Grafana Tempo).
   

  

 We’re scaling fast and staying true to what makes us different: an open-source legacy, a global collaborative culture, and a passion for meaningful work. Our team thrives in an innovation-driven environment where transparency, autonomy, and trust fuel everything we do.
   

  

 You may not meet every requirement, and that’s okay. If this role excites you, we’d love you to raise your hand for what could be a truly career-defining opportunity.
   

  

**Staff Backend Engineer - Adaptive Telemetry**
**This role is available for candidates located in the UK, Germany, Spain, Ireland and Sweden.**
**The Opportunity**
**What is Grafana Cloud?**
 Grafana Cloud is our composable observability platform that integrates metrics, logs, traces, and profiles with Grafana. It allows our customers to leverage the best open source observability software – including Prometheus, Mimir, Loki, Tempo, and Pyroscope – without the overhead of installing, maintaining and scaling their own observability stack.
   

  

 The Databases department owns and operates the telemetry databases that are Mimir for metrics, Loki for logs, Tempo for traces, and Pyroscope for profiles. We offer our databases as a Cloud service supporting Grafana Cloud.
   

  

**Adaptive Telemetry Group**
 The Adaptive Telemetry group, part of the Databases department, has the mission of ensuring that all telemetry stored in our databases is worthy of attention. Under that mission, the group is responsible for the development of Adaptive Metrics, Adaptive Logs, Adaptive Traces and Adaptive Profiles.
   

  

 Our Adaptive Telemetry solutions give users the ability to control and optimize their telemetry data. These solutions ensure that data storage is optimized based on individual usage patterns, so only the most valuable data is retained.
   

  

 As a company we are remote-first and global, we embrace people of different experiences and backgrounds to build diverse teams where every person brings a new perspective to the software.
   

  

**What Will You Be Doing**
* Drive technical strategy and roadmap. Proactively define the architectural vision, prioritize work that unlocks major product or platform improvements, and influence product and engineering decisions.
* Lead end-to-end delivery of large, cross-functional projects. Own planning, design, execution, rollout and long-term operation of large initiatives.
* Own architecture, reliability, performance and cost for critical systems. Make pragmatic architecture choices that balance scalability, availability, latency and cost while ensuring systems remain maintainable and evolvable.
* Define SLOs/SLIs and lead incident response. Establish measurable reliability targets, run high-severity incident response, lead blameless post-mortems, and drive systemic fixes and automation to prevent recurrence.
* Improve observability, automation and operational readiness. Champion telemetry, alerting, runbooks, capacity planning and automation efforts that reduce toil, speed debugging and lower MTTR.
* Align stakeholders and remove blockers. Coordinate across Product, Design and other teams to align priorities, negotiate tradeoffs, and unblock delivery for large initiatives.
* Mentor and grow engineering talent. Coach senior and mid-level engineers, lead design reviews, raise engineering standards, and help teammates make sound technical tradeoffs.
* Represent engineering internally and externally. Communicate technical strategy clearly to non-engineering stakeholders and represent the team in cross-team planning.


 We invest heavily in developer productivity. You can use modern AI coding assistants as part of your daily workflow (your choice of tools, within security guidelines), backed by a company-funded usage budget so you can iterate quickly without unnecessary friction.
   

  

 We encourage pragmatic AI-assisted development: faster prototyping, test generation, refactors, documentation, and incident follow-ups—always paired with strong code review and quality standards.
   

  

 You’ll also have access to frontier models (e.g., GPT-Codex 5/3, Claude Opus 4.6, Gemini 3 Pro).
   

  

**What Makes You a Great Fit**
**You are a motivated self starter with a bias towards action. You are customer focused. We build everything with our users in mind. You have a passion for creating intuitive products that fit customers’ needs**
* Proven delivery of large distributed systems. Experience shipping and operating complex systems that span multiple teams, with clear evidence of technical leadership and impact.
* Strong systems-design instincts. Deep understanding of tradeoffs around latency, consistency, availability, scaling and cost.
* Hands-on cloud and platform experience. Solid experience with cloud-native architectures (microservices, containers/Kubernetes, IaC) and the operational practices that keep them healthy.
* Reliability and performance ownership. Comfortable defining SLOs/SLIs, doing capacity planning, tuning performance, and driving reliability work end-to-end.
* Excellent coding and design skills. You write clear, maintainable, well-tested code and can lead technical designs — we use Go, but Python/C/C++/Rust or similar translate well.
* Comfort with AI-assisted development. We embrace AI and agentic development so we expect you to be curious and comfortable using AI-powered developer tools and ideally have practical experience folding them into a team’s workflow.
* Experience with messaging and telemetry. Familiarity with streaming/messaging systems (e.g., Kafka) and observability tooling (Prometheus/Grafana or equivalents).
* Influence without authority. Ability to align cross-functional stakeholders, set priorities and drive outcomes in a remote-first environment.
* Strong communicator. Clear written and verbal communication that works across engineers and non-technical stakeholders.


**Compensation & Rewards**
 In Sweden, the Base compensation range for this role is SEK 836,000 - SEK 1,045,000. Actual compensation may vary based on level, experience, and skillset as assessed in the interview process. Benefits include equity, bonus (if applicable) and other benefits listed here.
   

  

* Compensation ranges are country specific. If you are applying for this role from a different location than listed above, your recruiter will discuss your specific market’s defined pay range & benefits at the beginning of the process.


**Why You’ll Thrive At Grafana Labs**
* 100% Remote, Global Culture - As a remote-only company, we bring together talent from around the world, united by a culture of collaboration and shared purpose.
* Scaling Organization – Tackle meaningful work in a high-growth, ever-evolving environment.
* Transparent Communication – Expect open decision-making and regular company-wide updates.
* Innovation-Driven – Autonomy and support to ship great work and try new things.
* Open Source Roots – Built on community-driven values that shape how we work.
* Empowered Teams – High trust, low ego culture that values outcomes over optics.
* Career Growth Pathways – Defined opportunities to grow and develop your career.
* Approachable Leadership – Transparent execs who are involved, visible, and human.
* Passionate People – Join a team of smart, supportive folks who care deeply about what they do.
* In-Person onboarding - We want you to thrive from day 1 with your fellow new ‘Grafanistas’ to learn all about what we do and how we do it.
* Balance is Key - We operate a global annual leave policy of 30 days per annum. 3 days of your annual leave entitlement are reserved for Grafana Shutdown Days to allow the team to really disconnect. \*We will comply with local legislation where applicable.


**Equal Opportunity Employer:** 
 We will recruit, train, compensate and promote regardless of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, disability, age, veteran status, and all the other fascinating characteristics that make us different and unique. We believe that equality and diversity builds a strong organization and we’re working hard to make sure that’s the foundation of our organization as we grow.
   

  

*Grafana Labs may utilize AI tools in its recruitment process to assist in matching information provided in CVs to job postings. The recruitment team will continue to review inbound CVs manually to identify alignment with current openings.*
*For information about how your personal data is used once you’ve applied to a job, check out our privacy policy.*

title: Full Stack Engineer (Fintech)
description:
We’re looking for a Senior Full Stack Engineer with strong React expertise, solid backend experience, hands-on Supabase, and deep experience with Stripe integrations and financial transaction systems. This role is focused on building and scaling a fintech application where real-time payments, secure data handling, and seamless transaction flows are core to the product.  

  

**Responsibilities**  



  

* Build and maintain a full stack web application using React
* Develop and integrate REST APIs, webhooks, and third party services such as Stripe
* Design and manage PostgreSQL databases
* Implement secure authentication and authorization systems
* Ensure high performance for live, transaction based applications
* Sync data seamlessly between web and mobile platforms
* Maintain strong security practices around financial and sensitive user data
* Collaborate in a startup environment with a small, fast moving team
* 8+ years of software development experience
* Strong spoken and written English
* Advanced experience with React on the frontend
* Mid to senior level backend development experience
* Hands on experience with Supabase
* Experience with Stripe integrations on the backend
* Strong knowledge of REST APIs and webhooks
* Solid experience with PostgreSQL
* Experience building or maintaining real time or transaction driven applications
* Availability to work EST hours
* Fintech or payments industry experience will be a plus

title: Automotive Engineers | Stockholm
description:
**Are you passionate about technology, electrification and product development within the automotive industry? At Together Tech, we are seeing strong demand across several R&D assignments within electrified powertrain and HV inverter development.**


We are now looking to connect with driven engineers who want to work close to the technology and contribute to long‑term, high‑impact automotive projects. We work with leading players across passenger cars, heavy‑duty vehicles and e‑mobility, covering the full development cycle of electric drive systems, power electronics and related domains. Based on your technical background and career ambitions, we will match you with assignments where your competence makes an impact — while supporting your long‑term professional growth. We are currently looking for engineers within several engineering disciplines related to HV inverter and electric powertrain development, including:


**Mechanical Engineering**: Mechanical design of automotive inverters, including detailed component design, 3D modelling and drawing creation in SolidWorks. Responsibilities include design verification, requirement management, test cases and test reports, working closely with electronics, systems and test engineers.

**Test Engineering:** contribute to system‑ and product‑level testing within automotive development, including electrical and environmental testing, test specification development, execution and reporting, using laboratory equipment such as oscilloscopes, power meters and climate chambers and test automation with Robot Framework(Python) and LabVIEW.

**Hardware / Power Electronics Engineering**: Contribute to LV/HV automotive inverter development, including hardware design and verification, power stage and auxiliary circuit design, schematic capture in Cadence, simulation in LTspice, lab testing, troubleshooting and EMC‑aware documentation.  

**Application Engineer / SW Design Engineer:** act as a technical interface towards customers, combining customer support and product configuration with embedded software development and and troubleshooting, using C programming and Robot Framework(Python) together with tools such as Jenkins and Jira.

**Your technical bakgrund**

* A Bachelor's or Master's degree in a relevant field with several years of experience in the automotive industry.
* You have experience in one or more of the following areas: mechanical design within automotive applications, electrical or environmental testing and verification, embedded software development, or hardware and power electronics development.
* Good command of written and spoken English, proficiency in Swedish is considered an advantage.

**Your personal characteristics**  

Being a consultant puts extra light on having a flexible and curious mindset, a self-reliant way of working and your capability to build strong relationships with customers and colleagues, while keeping track of the target picture. You stay current with industry trends and emerging technologies and are motivated to develop together with us.

**Together Tech for sustainability**  

We provide opportunities for development through a variety of assignments in different industries, whether it be client projects or in one of our exciting in-house projects. At Together Tech, you work together with other engineers who are passionate about the environment and sustainability, all with a desire to create lasting solutions using technology - or as we say: Better World Tech!

**Your development & our support**  

Your development and well-being are important to us, and Together Tech offers benefits that support you and your career in the best way. We value the importance of secure employment and fixed salary, and we have a collective agreement. We prioritize your health and provide a wellness allowance of 4000 SEK per year. Moreover, we offer health check-ups, contributions to activities, and wellness discounts.


We enjoy spending time together through various social activities and team-building activities suitable for everyone! From after-works and sport activities to pentathlons and corporate conferences. Our commitment is to give you great conditions to grow through various assignments and projects as well as individual development plans. We also offer courses in sustainability and other courses within the framework of your role and goals. We take pride in being recognized as one of Sweden's most attractive employers in 2026, 2025 and 2024 by Karriärföretagen!


Learn more about us at www.togethertech.com.


For questions regarding the position, please contact Business Unit Manager Anna Öfwerman on +46790 738 026

title: Reverse Engineer
description:
#### **Why choose Coody**


At Coody, we cut out everything unnecessary to give more back to you as a consultant. And we have done it in the package of a lean and efficient organization, meaning no overpaid managers, no fancy offices and no extravagant conferences. Instead we give you:

**Maximized Compensation.** Typically a +40% salary increase.

**Freedom & Flexibility.** No bosses. All digital. Fully remote for those who prefer.

**Employment Security.** High salary, pension, insurance, paid vacation and a big educational fund.

**Shape the Future.** Projects and assignments for digital game changers like Spotify, Hemnet, Visa, Tobii, Toca Boca, Mojang, Flightradar24, SJ, and exciting startups and scaleups.

*As we are growing, we are continuously hiring senior tech consultants and connecting them with impactful projects through our client network.*

#### **What you'll be doing**


As a Senior Reverse Engineer at Coody, you'll take on deep technical assignments that require dissecting, understanding, and working within complex software systems — web, mobile, and beyond. Each assignment is typically 6–24 months, and as a consultant you're expected to ramp up quickly and deliver value from day one.


You'll be working in and around security-sensitive environments — analyzing applications, maintaining RE toolchains, and uncovering how systems behave under the hood. You'll own your work end-to-end: from setting up and maintaining reverse engineering environments to writing tooling that helps the teams you work with move faster and smarter.

#### **Who you are**


We are seeking a **Senior Reverse Engineer with at least 5+ years of experience** and a proven ability to get things done. Here's what we're seeking:

* Hands-on experience with reverse engineering of web and/or mobile applications using interactive disassemblers such as HexRays IDA, Ghidra, or Hopper.
* Practical experience with dynamic instrumentation and injection tools, particularly Frida, for hooking, bypassing security controls, and runtime analysis.
* Strong skills in traffic interception and analysis using tools like Charles Proxy and Wireshark.
* Experience maintaining and troubleshooting iOS or web-based RE environments, including in jailbroken contexts (e.g., using palera1n or similar jailbreak toolchains).
* Ability to build, maintain, and improve internal RE toolkits — you leave things better than you found them.
* Comfortable operating in ambiguous, low-documentation environments where your curiosity drives progress.
* **Based in Sweden:** You must have Swedish citizenship, a valid work visa, temporary or permanent residency.
* Fluent in English (spoken and written).

#### **This is Coody**


We're one of Sweden's fastest-growing tech consultancies, expanding in both people and revenue. We attribute our growth to our simple yet powerful approach to Gravity-Free Consulting — combining the freedom and flexibility of freelancing with the security of traditional employment.


At Coody, we believe in freedom, trust, and ambition, which means you'll feel like a freelancer with free rein on assignments that excite you, while enjoying the benefits of being employed by a company that invests in your growth.


Our senior sales team has built a vast, carefully curated client network, enabling our consultants to work with some of the most innovative companies in Sweden, Finland, the US, and the UK across Gaming, Fintech, Medtech, AI, SaaS, Aviation, and Automotive — tackling real technical challenges while shaping their own career paths.


We're a **diverse community** of ambitious tech professionals, representing more than **14 nationalities.**

**Coody is more of a platform than a traditional consultancy.** Everything we do is designed to help you work and live according to your own priorities and ambitions. If you're curious to see how this works in practice, explore our Employee Handbook and discover a company that truly puts consultants first.

#### **How to apply**


Are you excited to take your career to the next level and meet the criteria? Apply now, and we'll be in touch shortly.

title: Software Lead
description:
Location: Gothenburg, Sweden | Full-time | On-site  

  

**Bring your software engineering expertise to Freemelt, a deeptech company at the forefront of metal additive manufacturing. As a Software Lead, you will play a key role in developing the software platforms that steer, monitor and optimize our industrial 3d printing machines. In this role, you will lead architectural decisions, support and coordinate a skilled software team and collaborate closely with hardware, process, and systems engineering to deliver a robust, production ready industrial software.**

 **About Freemelt**  

Freemelt is an ambitious deeptech company developing cutting edge metal additive manufacturing solutions. Our proprietary Electron Beam Powder Bed Fusion (EPBF) technology enables customers to accelerate their development from research and feasibility studies to fullscale industrial production. We aim to become a leading productivity partner in the AM industry by delivering innovative, high-performance solutions for critical and regulated applications. As we expand into new markets and scale our technology, you will join a company shaping the future of industrial 3D printing.

 **About the role**  

As a Software Lead at Freemelt, you will lead the development of advanced software systems that control and optimize our metal 3D printing processes. In this role, you will lead and coach a team of 3-4 software engineers working with Scrum. As a team lead you will provide technical leadership, perform code reviews and define manufacturing software strategies for operational excellence. A central part of the role is to translate high-level product goals into clear software requirements and transform them into robust, production-ready implementations. You will guide and oversee the design of scalable, production-ready software for real-time monitoring, machine control and process improvement. You will work closely together with hardware and process engineers to secure seamless integration with our machines.  

This role is ideal for an experienced engineer who thrives in complex technical environments, enjoys balancing hands-on development with leadership and wants to influence software strategy while contributing to advanced industrial technology.

**Key responsibilities**

* Lead and manage a team of software engineers, provide technical guidance, coaching and fostering a collaborative Scrum environment
* Drive the Scrum methodology, sprint planning and roadmap execution to meet project goals and deliver robust industrial-grade solutions
* Develop applications by integrating front-end interfaces with back-end services for real-time machine monitoring and control
* Collaborate closely with mechanical, process and electrical engineers to ensure seamless hardware–software interaction
* Oversee and maintain strong code quality through reviews and best practices
* Maintain clear and compliant documentation
* Participate in the working group and contribute to architectural and strategic decisions regarding the company’s IT and server infrastructure together with our IT partner

 **Required qualifications**

* BSc or MSc in Computer Science or a related field
* 5+ years of experience in software development
* Strong experience with GNU/ Linuxbased systems, ideally Debian, used in embedded or industrial machine environments
* Proven experience using AI-assisted development tools (e.g. OpenAI Codex, Claude Code), combined with strong technical judgment
* Strong programming skills in Python and GoLang with experience working with software in advanced industrial machine systems
* Experience with realtime control systems, industrial protocols, or machine communication and monitoring using InfluxDB and Grafana
* Proven track record of leading software teams, driving agile Scrum practices and delivering complex projects in production environments

 **Preferred qualifications**

* Experience with C++ & GPU programming with OpenCL
* Experience developing React application
* Experience with AWS or Azure is a bonus

 **Why Freemelt**  

At Freemelt, you will join curious problem solvers and bold innovators who enjoy pushing ideas one step further. We work with passion, pace, and close collaboration — united by a drive to push industrial 3D printing forward. Here, your ideas matter. You will make a real impact on both our technology and our growth as we shape the future of metal additive manufacturing together.

 **Apply now**  

We review candidates continuously, so please send in your application as soon as possible.  

The final application date is 2026 04 30.


If you have any questions regarding the position, please contact Lars Löwgren, Engineering Lead,  

lars.lowgren@freemelt.com

  

Read more about Freemelt at our website and in social media:  

**www.freemelt.com**  

**LinkedIn**

title: Regulatory Architect
description:
**Take the lead in driving innovation**  

At Volvo Cars, we are committed to advancing safety, sustainability and electrification. To achieve our ambitious goals, we are looking for forward-thinking individuals who can identify challenges before they arise and deliver cutting-edge solutions that set new industry standards.  

  

When you choose a career with Volvo Cars, you’re not just joining a company – you’re helping shape the future of mobility.

**Let's introduce ourselves**
-----------------------------



At Architecture and Concept within the Software and Electronics unit, we define the overall system level architecture and concepts for the distributed software and electronics platform, including diagnostics and software applications.  

  

We are part of shaping Volvo Cars’ next generation software and electronics platform and create the foundation for a centralized computing system-enabling safe, secure, and scalable software driven functions across our future vehicles. By developing a high performance, multi node architecture built on advanced systems-on-chip, we provide a powerful platform for teams and partners to innovate and deploy the features that will define the cars of tomorrow.  

  

We are now looking for a Regulatory & Systems Engineer to strengthen our ability to meet both current and future needs by securing the tools, methods, and regulatory compliance across our vehicle programs and software ecosystem. This also includes understanding upcoming regulations and market developments to assess their impact on our architecture and systems, ensuring we stay ahead of requirements as our platform evolves.

**What you'll do**
------------------



You will join as a Regulatory and System Engineer and work within a cross-functional team alongside systems, concepts, and safety engineers. The team is responsible for defining and driving improvements and enabling solutions related to product structure, driving concepts, regulatory compliance, safety expertise, and governance.

  



Drive product regulation


* Monitor new and updated regulations and standards, identify trends, and proactively create structured awareness across the organization. Drive compliance with current and upcoming regulations affecting our software and electronics platform.
* Drive the Software and Electronics regulatory operating model at unit level by maintaining the Unit Regulatory Requirements Process and a recurring Regulatory Forum.
* Translate regulations into engineering implications by analyzing and breaking down regulatory requirements to identify their impact on embedded products, functions, platform software, and the system as a whole.
* Perform regulatory impact assessments on architecture and systems based on applicable requirements, including leading and executing regulatory impact analysis activities.
* Guide and cooperate with teams within the area of regulation, ensuring clarity, alignment, and understanding across stakeholders.
* Proactively guide program management on regulatory requirements, drive regulatory work packages and items, and clarify their implications for vehicle programs.
* Continuously strengthen ownership and responsibility within the unit and coordinate unit level regulatory signoff of applicable requirements.


Drive improvements in methods, product and realization


* Identify, define and drive improvements in the software and electronics product definition and realization structure, including supporting and enhancing the related processes.
* Ensure methods and tools supports technical integrity and secure these enables that solutions are documented in an efficient, structured, and traceable manner — from stakeholder requirements to architecture, and down to SW/HW development and verification.
* Continuously identify and drive efficiency improvements in our ways of working, methods, and tooling; measure progress using meaningful KPIs (e.g., coverage, traceability).
* Identify opportunities to apply AI-based methods and tools to improve quality, requirement handling, traceability, and overall efficiency in our engineering workflows.
**What you'll bring**
---------------------



We believe that you are a highly skilled Systems Engineer with several years of experience in designing and structure various different types of complex systems and solutions. You are a genuine problem solver with a positive and solution-oriented mindset. You are known to be a team player; you are curious, structured, and have a great ability to learn.  

  

Competence requirements and required experience:


* Good knowledge of systems engineering, architecture including embedded SW/HW
* Extensive knowledge and experience in requirements engineering, including working with requirement structures from system, software (SW), and hardware (HW) perspectives, as well as risk management.
* Strong background and understanding of regulatory requirements, including how they apply across different markets, interpretation of regulatory texts, and experience with regulatory authorities.
* Good knowledge of system safety engineering practices, safety standards, and product safety.
* M.Sc. in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering or equivalent
* Very good in English, both written and spoken
* Flexible, structured, meticulous, enthusiastic, methodical, analytical, and with high professional integrity
* Self-motivated and able to drive initiatives cross-functional from start to end
* Excellent collaboration skills

  



Preferable if you also have exposure to compliance driven development environments (e.g., automotive, aerospace, safety critical domains).

**Volvo Cars. For Life.**  

For nearly a century, Volvo Cars has empowered people to move freely in a personal, sustainable and safe way. Today, we are driving bold advancements in electrification, sustainability and automotive safety. To realise our ambitious vision, we are seeking innovative minds who are ready to tackle the challenges of tomorrow – today.
  

  

At Volvo Cars, we believe extraordinary things are achieved by ordinary people with a passion for making a difference. If you’re inspired by the opportunity to help redefine the future of mobility, we invite you to be part of our journey.  

  

**Ready to take the next step?**  

Submit your CV in English and tell us why you’re the ideal candidate for a role at Volvo Cars. Applications must be received no later than [XXth of XX XXX]. You will receive a confirmation email after your submission.
  

  

For questions regarding the recruitment process, please contact Recruiter Anita Mei Lin Noordzij at anita.noordzij@volvocars.com. For specific questions about the position, please reach out to Hiring Manager Simon Persson at SIMON.PERSSON@VOLVOCARS.COM.  

  

As part of the recruitment process, the final candidates will undergo a background check.
  

  

We welcome your application!



#LI-DNP
